entries.title: Cinematic spawn-on-train intro
entries.summary: You now begin each new world aboard the train. On your first arrival, a short cinematic sweeps up from the ground to reveal your train before handing you the controls — press Space at any time to skip it.
highlights: Spawn standing on a flatbed of the train instead of on the ground beside it
A fly-up intro camera reveals the train the first time you enter a world
Press Space to skip the intro (any other key shows the hint)

entries.title: Auto-publish CurseForge modpack after each release
entries.summary: Dungeon Train now ships as a CurseForge modpack that updates automatically about 15 minutes after every mod release, bundling the matching mod build plus the tested Sable dependency.
highlights: CurseForge modpack auto-follows every release
Bundles the tested Sable version and declares Dungeon Train's dependencies

entries.title: Enemies scale with difficulty level
entries.summary: Train enemies now spawn based on the current difficulty. Each spawn egg has a difficulty range, so the early game stays gentle — small slimes and basic mobs — while tougher foes like husks, bogged and vindicators ramp in only as you travel deeper and the difficulty climbs. When an enemy is out of its range, that spot fills with an in-range enemy instead, so enemy carriages and cages are never left empty.
highlights: Easy mobs (small slimes) are gated to the opening levels; tougher mobs appear only at higher difficulty
Tuned difficulty windows across the skeleton, bogged, pillager, vindicator, zombie and slime carriages
Editors can set a per-egg min/max difficulty band in the block-variant menu (shift-click the max down to 'all')
Slimes are now an early-game threat: small slimes spawn first and taper off as difficulty climbs
Ranged skeletons, husks, bogged, pillager patrols and vindicators ramp in deeper, each on its own difficulty window
Added enemy variety and new spawn spots to the enemies and slime carriages, and re-tuned the caged-zombie spawns

entries.title: Advancements only earnable in survival or adventure
entries.summary: Dungeon Train advancements can no longer be earned in creative or spectator mode — play in survival or adventure to unlock them. The in-game editor's advancements still unlock in creative, and vanilla Minecraft advancements are unaffected.
highlights: DT gameplay advancements can't be earned in creative or spectator
Editor advancements still unlock in creative
Vanilla and other-mod advancements unchanged

entries.title: Carriage spawn-egg mobs arrive armed
entries.summary: Mobs you place in a carriage with spawn eggs now spawn with their normal gear. Pillagers carry a crossbow (and occasionally the raid-captain's ominous banner); skeletons and bogged carry a bow. As the train's difficulty climbs, ranged mobs keep their bow or crossbow and have it enchanted, instead of being handed a melee weapon that left them defenceless at range.
highlights: Spawn-egg pillagers spawn with a crossbow, with a chance at the ominous banner
Spawn-egg skeletons and bogged spawn with a bow
Higher difficulty now enchants a ranged mob's bow/crossbow instead of replacing it

entries.title: Piglins no longer wrongly become magma cubes outside the Nether
entries.summary: On a train's easy opening stretch, tough mobs get swapped for friendly slimes (and Nether creatures for magma cubes). Piglins were being turned into magma cubes even on Overworld trains. Now zombified piglins are never swapped, and piglins and piglin brutes only turn into magma cubes inside the Nether — on other trains they spawn exactly as placed.
highlights: Zombified piglins are never replaced — they always spawn as placed
Piglins & piglin brutes only become magma cubes on Nether trains
Overworld trains no longer spawn out-of-place magma cubes from piglins

entries.title: Compatible Terrain mode + optional Distant Horizons & Tectonic support
entries.summary: A new Compatible Terrain option lets Dungeon Train generate vanilla-style overworlds, so terrain mods like Tectonic and Terralith - and Distant Horizons' long-range views - work with the train. It is off by default (classic Dungeon Train terrain stays the default); switch it on in Dungeon Train settings before creating a world. The modpack now offers Distant Horizons and Tectonic as optional add-ons you can enable at install (use Distant Horizons 2.x).
highlights: New Compatible Terrain option - run the train across Tectonic / Terralith terrain
Distant Horizons + Tectonic offered as opt-in modpack add-ons (never forced)
Classic terrain unchanged by default; existing worlds unaffected

entries.title: Leatherworker shop carriage
entries.summary: A leatherworker's stall now rides the train among the other shops. Its chest is stocked with leather and the full set of leather armour, a whole rainbow of dyes, emeralds, a potion and other odds and ends from the tannery — handy for patching together armour or recolouring your kit mid-run.
highlights: New leatherworker shop carriage joins the train's rotating shops
Stocked with leather, the full leather-armour set and leather horse armour
A complete rainbow of dyes, plus emeralds, a cauldron, a potion and a water bucket

entries.title: Reliable spawn on the train's front deck
entries.summary: Joining or respawning onto the moving train now reliably lands you standing on the front flatbed deck. Before, if the train hadn't fully settled or had already grown past its first carriage, you could occasionally drop through to the tracks under the train or get stuck inside the deck floor.
highlights: Always spawn cleanly on the train's front deck
No more falling under the train or clipping into the flatbed slab on join or respawn

entries.title: Empty enemy spots no longer leave a stray block behind
entries.summary: A carriage spot that can only hold enemies — and whose enemies are all the wrong level for your current depth — now stays as empty air instead of leaving a stray block where a mob would have stood. The enemy still appears once you travel deep enough for it to belong.
highlights: Mob-only carriage spots with no depth-appropriate enemy now clear to air
No more leftover placeholder block where a not-yet-eligible mob would stand

entries.title: Advancements keybind reminder in chat
entries.summary: Earn a Dungeon Train advancement before you've ever opened the advancements screen and a short, train-flavoured message now points you at the key that opens it. It shows your actual (rebindable) keybind, picks at random from a dozen conductor-style lines, and quietly stops for good once you open the advancements screen.
highlights: Chat reminder of the key that opens your advancements
Shows your real, rebindable keybind (not a hardcoded key)
Random from 12 train-flavoured lines; stops once you open the screen

entries.title: Five train-traversal advancements
entries.summary: Five new advancements reward exploring the world the train rides through — hopping down onto the tracks, wandering off and finding your way back aboard, and discovering the bridge-pillar staircases and tunnel stairwells along the route.
highlights: Track Record — set foot on the rails below the train
Off the Rails — leave the train and tracks behind for the open world
Back on Track — climb aboard again after getting off
Taking the Stairs — use a bridge-pillar staircase
Beneath the Candy Kingdom — enter a tunnel stairwell

entries.title: Armor stands as randomized carriage contents
entries.summary: Carriage cells can now randomly feature an armor stand wearing a rolled set of armor, sitting alongside the usual blocks and loot. Some carriages show an equipped stand in a corner, others leave it empty — more variety in what you find aboard the train.
highlights: Armor stands can now appear as random carriage contents, sometimes wearing a full set of gear
Each stand's loadout is rolled per spawn, so no two carriages are guaranteed alike

entries.title: Keep Inventory now carries your gear to the next world
entries.summary: With Keep Inventory turned on, dying and starting a New World now brings your whole inventory and experience along — and the new world keeps the rule on, so your gear and XP carry through run after run. With Keep Inventory off, nothing changes.
highlights: Inventory (including armor and offhand) and XP carry into the next world when Keep Inventory is on
The next world keeps the Keep Inventory rule enabled automatically, so it chains across every run
No change when Keep Inventory is off — the next world still starts empty

entries.title: Quality-of-life mods in the modpack: AppleSkin + Mouse Tweaks
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train modpack now includes AppleSkin — the food saturation and hunger-value overlay — enabled by default (you can untick it when installing). Mouse Tweaks, for faster inventory management (shift-drag and scroll-to-move items), is offered as an optional add-on you can opt into.
highlights: AppleSkin food/saturation overlay now ships with the modpack (on by default)
Mouse Tweaks inventory quality-of-life available as an optional add-on

entries.title: Know The Rules advancement + clearer ride-time goals
entries.summary: A new "Know The Rules" advancement joins the Taking Notes branch — read every Quiet Rule of the train to earn it, and "Respect The Rules" now nests beneath it. Two ride-time advancements also spell out how long you need to stay aboard: "Settling In" (ten hours) and "I Live Here Now" (twenty-four hours).
highlights: New "Know The Rules" advancement — read every Quiet Rule of the train
"Respect The Rules" now sits beneath it in the Taking Notes branch
"Settling In" and "I Live Here Now" descriptions now state the hours required

entries.title: More Quiet Rules of the Train — the maker, the key, and fellow adventurers
entries.summary: The Quiet Rules of the Train book gains ten new entries. Some whisper of reaching the one who built the train; one points to the key that reveals what you came here to do — and shows your own bound advancements key inline; the rest are hard-won advice on the other adventurers you meet: when to bow, when to gift, when to defend, and how to win them over.
highlights: A rule on reaching the train's maker via "@Dev"
A rule that reveals your own advancements key, inline in the page
Seven tips on befriending (or surviving) fellow adventurers

entries.title: Jade tooltips available as an optional modpack add-on
entries.summary: Jade — the "what am I looking at" overlay that shows the name and details of whatever block or item you're aiming at — is now offered as an optional add-on for the Dungeon Train modpack that you can opt into. Heads-up: Jade's tooltip doesn't show for blocks on the moving train yet (a Sable limitation); it works everywhere else.
highlights: Jade block & item tooltips offered as an optional modpack add-on
Known limitation: no tooltip for blocks on the moving train (works fine off-train)

entries.title: Type @Dev in chat: autocomplete + highlight
entries.summary: The bundled Discord Presence mod updates to 0.15.0. Typing @Dev (or @brennanhatton) in chat now autocompletes like a command and turns yellow as you type — so the new Quiet Rule about reaching the maker is something you can actually see and use. The deeper Discord wiring (real pings, engaged-only relay) ships too but stays off until a server owner turns it on.
highlights: @Dev / @brennanhatton autocomplete in chat (Tab to complete) and highlight yellow as you type
Pairs with the new “reach the maker via @Dev” Quiet Rule
Real Discord pings and relay gating are bundled but off by default

entries.title: Meet, befriend, or lay to rest your own echo
entries.summary: Three new advancements follow your reunions with your own echo — a passenger reincarnated from one of your past lives. Walk up to one, win it over with a two-way gift exchange, or put it to rest.
highlights: New "Hello, Old Soul" — come within two blocks of your own echo
"Old Friends, New Life" — befriend your own echo
"Lay to Rest" — kill your own echo

entries.title: AppleSkin listed as an optional dependency
entries.summary: AppleSkin — the food and saturation overlay bundled on-by-default in the Dungeon Train modpack — is now also listed as an optional add-on on the mod's own CurseForge page, so it's discoverable straight from the mod.
highlights: AppleSkin now shown as an optional dependency of the mod

entries.title: Archways fade between elevated track pillars
entries.summary: Where the train's track climbs onto tall support pillars — crossing ravines, rivers, and oceans — the pillars now fade toward each other with stepped archways instead of standing as bare posts. The arches are built from each pillar's own stone, so a viaduct made of fancy or themed pillars arches in its own material.
highlights: Tall track pillars now fade into stepped archways toward their neighbours
Arches copy each pillar's own blocks, so themed pillars arch in-material

entries.title: Everything Burrito: earn every other advancement
entries.summary: A new challenge advancement, Everything Burrito, unlocks once you've earned every other Dungeon Train advancement — editor achievements aside. It hangs off 'Help I Can't Get Off' at the end of the line, and tracks the achievement list dynamically, so it always means '100%' even as new advancements arrive in future updates.
highlights: New 'Everything Burrito' challenge advancement for 100% completion
Counts every non-editor advancement — and auto-includes any added in future updates

entries.title: Pause menu works during the intro cutscene
entries.summary: Switching windows (or pressing Esc) during the spawn intro cutscene now opens a pause menu you can actually use — you can click its buttons and Esc closes it — and the cutscene picks up right where it left off when you come back. Previously the menu could open in a frozen, unclickable state.
highlights: The pause menu is now clickable and closable during the intro cutscene
The intro now pauses and resumes around the menu instead of locking up

entries.title: New decor carriages (house, tree, cherry blossom) + broken roof
entries.summary: A new 'features' content set rides the train — house, tree, and cherry-blossom builds that can appear inside several carriage types, with chests of loot in the tree and cherry-blossom variants. Cracked carriages also gain a new 'broken' roof look.
highlights: New house, tree, and cherry-blossom carriage content (tree & cherry blossom carry loot chests)
New 'broken' roof variant for cracked carriages

entries.title: Fixed players spawning under the train
entries.summary: Spawning or respawning onto the train no longer occasionally drops you through the deck and leaves you stuck underneath. On busy world loads the client could free-fall off the deck before the train's collision finished loading; the game now holds you on the deck through that window so you always start standing on the train.
highlights: No more falling under the train on login, respawn, or world reload

entries.title: Gentler opening: a mob-free start, then slimes, then normal
entries.summary: New runs now ease in over three stages. The first stretch of the train is completely free of hostile mobs, so you can find your feet, meet the villagers and grab starter loot in peace. After that, a stretch of harmless slimes gives you a gentle combat warm-up before the usual mobs and difficulty kick in. It's all on by default and fully tunable per world.
highlights: The first ~10 carriages spawn no hostile mobs — villagers, traders and friendly mobs still ride along
The next ~15 carriages spawn only slimes, for an easy combat warm-up
Normal mobs and the usual difficulty curve resume after the opening stretch
All three stages are on by default and tunable in dungeontrain-server.toml

entries.title: Editor welcome message
entries.summary: Opening the in-game editor now greets you with a short welcome that points out what the editor can do and how to reach Brennan for help.
highlights: A friendly welcome appears in chat a couple of seconds after you first open the editor each session.
Click the @brennanhatton mention to message Brennan directly — your message reaches him on Discord.

entries.title: Tag the dev from in-game chat
entries.summary: Type @dev or @brennanhatton in chat and it now pings the developer in the community Discord — a message shouted into the dark can finally reach someone.
highlights: @dev and @brennanhatton in chat now ping the developer in the community Discord feed

entries.title: Death-screen feedback survey
entries.summary: When you die, a Give Feedback button opens a short survey: rate your run 0-10 with an optional comment, then answer how the difficulty progression feels. Answers post to the community Discord. It walks the questions one at a time and never re-asks one you've already answered.
highlights: Give Feedback button on the death screen
0-10 ratings with optional comments, sent to Discord
Asks how the difficulty progression is feeling

entries.title: Editor welcome shows Brennan's Discord "last seen online"
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train editor welcome can again end with whether Brennan is online on Discord right now, or how long ago he was last seen — so creators know if a quick reply is likely. Powered by Discord Presence's presence query over the community relay; gracefully hidden when his presence is unknown.
highlights: Editor welcome shows "Brennan is online on Discord right now!" or "last seen online X ago"
Gracefully hidden when his Discord presence is unknown

entries.title: Fewer trees in carriage contents
entries.summary: Reduced the tree feature's spawn weight in carriage contents, so trees appear less frequently relative to other decorative features.

entries.title: All advancements now persist across worlds
entries.summary: Every advancement you earn — vanilla Minecraft (Story, Nether, The End, Adventure, Husbandry), Dungeon Train, and other mods — now follows you into every world, the same way Dungeon Train's own advancements always have. Start a brand-new world and your earned advancements are already unlocked, no fanfare. Advancements you'd earned before this update get absorbed automatically the next time you load an existing world. Recipe-book unlocks stay per-world.
highlights: Vanilla and other-mod advancements now carry across worlds, not just Dungeon Train's
Earned advancements from existing worlds are absorbed automatically on next load
Recipe-book unlocks stay per-world

entries.title: Tagging @dev in chat shows how recently Brennan was online
entries.summary: Type @dev or @brennanhatton in chat and the game quietly tells you how recently Brennan was last online on Discord — "online right now" or "online 5 minutes ago" — so you know whether to expect a reply. It only appears when he's been around in the last half hour, lands a beat after your own message, and won't show more than once every 5 minutes.
highlights: Tag @dev or @brennanhatton to see Brennan's recent online status in chat
Only shows when he was online within the last 30 minutes
Quietly rate-limited to once every 5 minutes, so it never spams

entries.title: "Reboarder" advancement now unlocks reliably
entries.summary: Shoving a passenger off the moving train now reliably earns the Reboarder advancement — including when they tumble off the open side of a carriage into a gap or fall onto the tracks below, not just when they're knocked cleanly clear. Those off-the-edge falls used to slip through and never counted.
highlights: Pushing a passenger off the train now reliably unlocks Reboarder
Counts passengers that fall off the side or onto the tracks, not only clean knock-offs

entries.title: New book: "All the Creator Asks"
entries.summary: A new random book joins the pool — a fallen adventurer's reverent testament that treats the death-screen feedback survey as a holy rite of the afterlife, written to gently beg you to answer it. Eight short variants turn up in chests, on bookshelves, and at narrative lecterns.
highlights: New 8-variant random book that venerates the feedback survey as a spiritual passage
Found in chest rolls, bookshelf drops, and narrative lecterns

entries.title: Replay the intro cinematic on demand
entries.summary: The opening cinematic is no longer a one-time thing — run /dungeontrain cinematic (or /dt cinematic) any time to watch it again. By default the camera lifts off from a spot on the ground beside the track, right where you are, and sweeps up and away as the train carries you onward — just like the intro. Prefer to start from exactly where you're looking? Use /dt cinematic current.
highlights: New /dt cinematic command replays the opening cinematic on demand
Default shot rises from the ground beside you near the track; 'current' starts from your current view

entries.title: Carriage # and difficulty on Discord advancement posts
entries.summary: Earn an advancement and the community Discord now shows which carriage you were riding and your difficulty level at that moment, posted on its own line right below the advancement.
highlights: Reads e.g. "Carriage +7 · Difficulty Level 3", matching the in-game HUD.

entries.title: New advancement: The Great Beyond
entries.summary: Earn a new advancement — "The Great Beyond" — by completing the death-screen feedback survey. Once you've answered every survey question, it unlocks. The survey is provided by the bundled Discord Presence mod, which now also lets you open it any time with the new /feedback command.
highlights: "The Great Beyond" — Complete the feedback survey after death.
Counts however you finish the survey — on the death screen or via /feedback.

entries.title: Time-aboard & distance-ridden advancements
entries.summary: Seven new advancements reward the long haul. Stay aboard for an hour or two without dying, and rack up the kilometres you ride — both within a single life and across every run you've ever taken. They branch off Dungeon Train Explorer in the advancements screen.
highlights: Single-life time aboard: One Long Ride (1 hour) and Reality Escaped (2 hours)
Single-life distance: Escaping Spawn (10 km), Cross-Country (100 km), The Long Run (250 km)
Lifetime distance: Covering ground (100 km) and I got so far (1,000 km)

entries.title: Narrative death screen
entries.summary: Dying on the train now opens a paginated, narrated retrospective instead of the plain vanilla screen. The Dungeon Train reflects on your fall, your deeds, the cargo you carried, all your lives, and the platform ahead — your run and lifetime stats woven in, a train that fills as you read, and lore that adapts to how you died. Page through with Continue, then Board anew or Leave the line.
highlights: Paginated narrative death screen with data-driven lore
Per-run and lifetime stats: carriages, distance, time aboard, mobs, friends, loot containers, books, deaths
A train graphic that fills carriage by carriage as you advance
Optional end-of-run feedback survey, then Board anew or Leave the line

entries.title: Disable vanilla movement tutorial on world creation
entries.summary: The vanilla Minecraft movement tutorial (WASD / look around / jump hints in the top-right corner) no longer appears when starting a new Dungeon Train world. These hints are irrelevant in DT and conflicted with the cinematic intro.
highlights: Vanilla WASD / look / jump tutorial hints no longer appear on world start

entries.title: TrainCinematographer mode
entries.summary: New spectator mode for filming dungeon runs. Enter with /dungeontrain cinematographer; nearby doors on the train auto-open as you approach. Press C to replay the cinematic intro. The DT branch HUD hides automatically while active.

entries.title: Cinematographer mode only opens doors now
entries.summary: The spectator filming mode added in 0.316.0 was also swinging open trapdoors, fence gates and barrels as the camera drifted past. It now opens only actual doors, so set dressing and storage stay put while you film.
highlights: Trapdoors, fence gates and barrels no longer flip open in cinematographer mode
Only real doors auto-open as the camera approaches

entries.title: Persistent Ender Chest across runs
entries.summary: The Ender Chest now persists between worlds — items you store survive your death and carry into the next run. Survival and creative inventories are kept separate, so switching game modes gives you a fresh Ender Chest for that mode.
highlights: Ender Chest contents carry forward when you die and start a new world
Separate inventory per game mode (survival, creative, adventure, spectator)
Swaps live when you switch game modes mid-session

entries.title: Death screen: lectern stories count as books, plus first-death send-off
entries.summary: Stories you read at narrative lecterns now count toward the "Books read" tally on your death-screen run summary — previously only books read from your hand were counted. And your very first death now gets its own bespoke lines on the "all your lives" and "the platform" pages, instead of the generic send-off written for veteran riders.
highlights: Narrative-lectern stories now count toward "Books read" on the death screen
Unique first-death dialogue on the "all your lives" and "the platform" pages

entries.title: Cinematographer clear-view: see through walls to film the train
entries.summary: TrainCinematographer mode gains a clear-view sub-mode: while filming, the blocks directly in front of the camera turn invisible so terrain, carriage walls and clutter stop blocking the shot — then quietly return as you pan away, leaving the world untouched. Doors, trapdoors and slabs are left alone, and the camera's automatic door-opening now reaches much further down the train.
highlights: New: /dt cinematographer clearview on|off — see straight through whatever the camera is pointed at
Blocks turn invisible (not removed), so nothing collapses and your train stays intact — decorated pots, bookshelves and chests come back with their contents
Cinematographer now auto-opens doors much further ahead (50 blocks)
